big_idea_statement: Golf has always said 'it's the archer, not the arrow.' But what if the arrow has been bent this whole time? Every putter you've ever owned puts the weight behind the shaft — creating a gravitational force that drifts the face open on every stroke. Your hands have been compensating for a physics problem no one told you existed. The miss was never mental. It was mechanical. RS1 moves the weight forward. The arrow is finally straight.

metadata.arena_note: Condensed Arena (5 competitors x 2 rounds) per user instruction. The Bent Arrow concept won decisively across all evaluation dimensions. Self-approved — user sleeping per overnight execution protocol.

concept_candidates.plain_language_statement: Golf's deepest belief — 'it's the archer, not the arrow' — is wrong when it comes to putting. The arrow (the putter) has had a hidden flaw this whole time: Rear Axis Weighting that drifts the face open. The golfer was never the problem. The equipment was.
concept_candidates.rc_angle: Challenges the foundational belief that equipment is neutral and the golfer is the variable. Uses the root cause (Rear Axis Weighting) to prove the arrow was literally bent.
concept_candidates.mechanism_angle: Forward Axis Weighting IS the straightened arrow. Moving the weight forward corrects the flaw that has existed in every putter.
concept_candidates.promise_angle: When the arrow is straight, the archer's aim finally shows in the result. Your putting matches your ball-striking.
concept_candidates.schema_violation: Violates golf's deepest cultural norm: never blame the equipment. This idea says the equipment WAS the problem — and proves it with physics.
concept_candidates.nr_potential: Extremely high. Explains the golfer's entire putting history: every miss, every carousel purchase, every honeymoon that ended, every pressure putt that failed. All of it was the bent arrow.
concept_candidates.soul_alignment: Quiet power. The reframe is delivered as a discovered truth, not a marketing claim. 'The arrow has been bent this whole time' has the restrained authority of Soul.md.
concept_candidates.why_this_works: This Big Idea works because it inverts golf's most sacred belief with provable physics. It simultaneously liberates the golfer from decades of self-blame AND creates a new evaluation framework (where is the weight?) that only RS1 satisfies. The metaphor is instantly graspable, memorable, and carries the full weight of the root cause and mechanism in a single image.

concept_candidates.plain_language_statement: There are three generations of putter design. Generation 1: putters that flopped open. Generation 2: putters that stopped the flop but left you alone (LAB). Generation 3: putters that actively work with your stroke. RS1 is the first Generation 3 putter.
concept_candidates.rc_angle: The root cause persisted across Gen 1 and wasn't fully solved by Gen 2. Only Gen 3 addresses both the equipment flaw AND the technique requirement.
concept_candidates.mechanism_angle: Forward Axis Weighting is the Gen 3 technology — active face squaring through gravitational reversal, plus technique assist.
concept_candidates.promise_angle: The evolution was always heading here. The putter that does more so you do less.
concept_candidates.schema_violation: Moderate — reframes the competitive landscape as evolutionary stages rather than brand comparison. Makes LAB look like a waypoint, not a destination.
concept_candidates.nr_potential: Good. Explains why LAB was an improvement but not the final answer. Explains the carousel as being stuck in Gen 1.
concept_candidates.soul_alignment: Fits the evolutionary narrative directive from Soul.md. Respectful of LAB while positioning RS1 as the next chapter.
concept_candidates.why_this_works: Creates a category framework that positions RS1 at the top of an evolutionary ladder. Less emotionally powerful than The Bent Arrow but more structurally useful for the eCom page.

concept_candidates.plain_language_statement: The reason your practice-green putting doesn't transfer to the course is not mental weakness. It's a physics interaction: pressure tightens your grip, and your putter turns that extra grip pressure into face angle error because the weight is behind the shaft. Remove the backward weight, and the practice-to-course gap closes.
concept_candidates.rc_angle: Uses the practice-to-course gap — the most universally experienced putting frustration — as the entry point for the root cause revelation.
concept_candidates.mechanism_angle: Forward Axis Weighting removes the variable that pressure exploits (backward CG). Physics don't change under pressure.
concept_candidates.promise_angle: What you can do on the practice green, you can do on the course. The gap was never in your head.
concept_candidates.schema_violation: Strong — reframes a universally accepted 'mental toughness' explanation as a physics problem with a physics solution.
concept_candidates.nr_potential: Very high for golfers who experience the practice-to-course gap. Explains their specific history of frustration.
concept_candidates.soul_alignment: Technical precision. Engineer explaining a phenomenon the golfer has experienced.
concept_candidates.why_this_works: Targets the specific moment of frustration every golfer recognizes. Less universal than The Bent Arrow but more immediately relatable for the primary persona.

concept_candidates.plain_language_statement: You don't have the yips. Your putter has a physics problem that your nervous system is solving in real time. The twitch, the deceleration, the steering — that's your hands compensating for a face that drifts open. Your body isn't broken. The weight is in the wrong place.
concept_candidates.rc_angle: The yips are reframed from a neurological/psychological condition to a rational neuromuscular response to equipment physics.
concept_candidates.mechanism_angle: Forward Axis Weighting removes the stimulus that triggers the yips response. No backward CG = no face drift = no need for panicked hand correction.
concept_candidates.promise_angle: Dignity restoration. You're not broken. The putter was.
concept_candidates.schema_violation: Very high — challenges the medical/psychological framing of a condition golfers consider permanent.
concept_candidates.nr_potential: Extremely high for yips sufferers. Reframes their entire experience from disorder to rational response.
concept_candidates.soul_alignment: Fits the scientific discovery register. Restrained delivery of a potentially life-changing reframe.
concept_candidates.why_this_works: Highest emotional impact for yips sufferers. Risks being too narrow for general audience, but powerful as a secondary angle or email/ad hook.

concept_candidates.plain_language_statement: Every tool humans use puts the heavy end forward. Hammers. Axes. Pendulums. The physics are obvious: heavy end forward means gravity does the work. Except putters. For decades, putters have put the heavy end behind the pivot point and asked your hands to fight gravity on every stroke. RS1 is the first putter built the way every other tool is built: heavy end forward.
concept_candidates.rc_angle: Reveals the root cause through an analogy so obvious it creates the 'why didn't anyone do this before?' response.
concept_candidates.mechanism_angle: Forward Axis Weighting framed as the most natural engineering principle — not innovation, but correction of a decades-old oversight.
concept_candidates.promise_angle: The putter finally works the way every other tool works. Gravity does the work.
concept_candidates.schema_violation: Moderate — the 'obvious in hindsight' framing creates surprise through simplicity rather than complexity.
concept_candidates.nr_potential: Good. The 'every other tool' comparison explains why all previous putters felt wrong.
concept_candidates.soul_alignment: Strong engineering voice. Chris McGinley energy — practical, clear, authoritative.
concept_candidates.why_this_works: Creates the 'why didn't anyone do this before?' reaction that signals genuine innovation. Less emotionally charged than The Bent Arrow but highly effective for the technical/analytical audience segment.

concept_candidates.plain_language_statement: Let it fall. Two words that change everything about how you putt. When the weight is in front, the putter falls toward the ball on the correct path — gravity does the steering. You don't push it. You don't guide it. You let it fall. The tagline is the technique.
concept_candidates.fssit_anchor: FSSIT-01 (self-blame liberation — the technique IS doing less)
concept_candidates.rc_angle: The root cause reframe distilled to two words: 'let it fall' implies that you've been doing too much (because the putter required it).
concept_candidates.mechanism_angle: Forward Axis Weighting literally enables the 'let it fall' technique. Heavy front = gravity pulls it through.
concept_candidates.promise_angle: Do less. Make more. The tagline is the promise is the technique.
concept_candidates.schema_violation: Moderate — violates the expectation that putting improvement requires more effort, more practice, more technique.
concept_candidates.nr_potential: Moderate. Less past-explaining than The Bent Arrow but more forward-looking.
concept_candidates.soul_alignment: Perfect fit. Minimal, restrained, confident. Two words that carry the entire campaign.
concept_candidates.why_this_works: The tagline IS the Big Idea. 'Let it fall' works as instruction, as campaign platform, as product demo description, and as emotional resolution. Maximum compression of the full strategic synthesis.

concept_phase.concept_selection_rationale: BI-01 scored highest on narrative reorganization potential (explains the golfer's entire putting history), FSSIT alignment (directly crystallizes the Technique Deflection, the highest-magnitude latent emotion at 0.85), schema distance (6.5 — optimal surprise zone), and Soul.md alignment (quiet power, earned authority). It also uniquely inverts golf's deepest belief ('it's the archer, not the arrow') — a cultural move that no competitor can replicate because only RS1 can prove the arrow was bent (Forward Axis Weighting + FDB demo).
concept_phase.concept_approved_rationale: The Bent Arrow concept was partially seeded in the root cause downstream handoffs ('The arrow has been bent this whole time' is already an anchor phrase). It represents the natural synthesis of root cause + mechanism + promise into a single metaphor that carries the full campaign. Additionally, BI-06 ('Let it fall') is used as the campaign tagline platform rather than as the Big Idea itself — the tagline and the Big Idea work in tandem.

creative_wrapping.big_idea_type_rationale: The enemy is not a person or company — it is a physics flaw that has been hidden in every putter. The 'enemy' is Rear Axis Weighting, personified through the metaphor of a bent arrow. This fits the enemy Big Idea type because it identifies a hidden antagonist (backward weight) that has been sabotaging the golfer without their knowledge, then reveals the solution (Forward Axis Weighting) as the weapon that defeats the enemy.
headlines: The Arrow Has Been Bent This Whole Time.
That Miss Wasn't Mental. It Was Mechanical.
Every Putter You've Owned Had the Weight in the Wrong Place.
Golf Told You It Was the Archer. It Was the Arrow.
Your Hands Have Been Fighting Physics on Every Putt.
Place It on a Table. Watch the Face Sit Down. That's the Difference.
The Only Putter Gravity Works With, Not Against.
75% of the Weight. In 25% of the Head.
What Happens When You Stop Fighting the Putter.
Roll Straight. The Name Is the Promise.

leads.lead_text:
You know the feeling.

Four-footer for par. Your hands feel different. Tighter.
You make your stroke. It feels decent.
The ball slides past the right edge.

Golf has always said: 'It's the archer, not the arrow.'
But what if the arrow has been bent this whole time?

Virtually every putter ever made puts its weight behind the shaft.
That weight creates a gravitational force that drifts the face open during your stroke.
Your hands fight it. Every putt. Every stroke.
Under pressure — when your grip tightens — they can't fight it enough.

That miss wasn't mental. It was mechanical.

And now there's a putter that moves the weight forward.
So gravity works with the face, not against it.
So your hands can stop fighting.

Let it fall.
leads.fssit_connection: FSSIT-01 direct delivery: crystallizes the self-blame liberation + equipment reframe

leads.lead_text:
Place any putter you own face-down on a flat surface.
The face will flip up. Or roll to the side.
Every putter does this. Every single one.

Now place the RS1 on the same surface.
The face stays down.

That's not a gimmick. That's 75% of the head weight sitting in the front 25% of the putter.
It's called Face Down Balance. And it's the visible proof of something that changes how you think about every putter you've ever owned.

The weight in your putter has always been behind the shaft.
Pulling the face open. Making your hands compensate.
RS1 puts the weight in front.
Gravity does the work.

The face stays square because physics says it should.
leads.fssit_connection: FSSIT-02 support: demonstrates the design flaw that explains the carousel

leads.lead_text:
Chris McGinley has spent 25 years designing putters for the world's best golfers.
He's built clubs for 11 world #1 players.
He's worked inside the labs at the biggest OEMs in golf.

And he'll tell you something that might surprise you.

Virtually every putter — from the $50 starter to the $500 Scotty — puts its weight in the same place. Behind the shaft.

That's always been the convention. And it's always been wrong.

When the weight sits behind the shaft, gravity pulls the face open during your stroke.
Every putter you've ever owned had this problem.
The only difference was the shape.

McGinley spent three years building the first putter that puts the weight where it should be.
In front of the shaft.

He calls it Forward Axis Weighting.
The putter calls it Roll Straight.
leads.fssit_connection: FSSIT-01 support: authority figure confirming that the equipment was the problem

campaign_platform.tagline_meaning: Double entendre: (1) Let gravity pull the putter through the stroke. (2) Let the ball fall in the hole. The tagline is both technique instruction and promise.
campaign_platform.big_idea_anchor: The arrow has been bent this whole time.
campaign_platform.platform_coherence: Let it fall (tagline) → because the arrow has been bent (big idea) → Forward Axis Weighting straightens it (mechanism) → gravity-driven, face-controlled (product truth) → do less, make more (promise) → your putting matches the rest of your game (transformation)

dimension_scores.resonance_notes: Direct FSSIT-01 delivery (impact 0.91). Addresses the Technique Deflection (ELG-01, magnitude 0.85) — the highest-impact latent emotion. The bent arrow metaphor crystallizes decades of unspoken self-blame into a single image.
dimension_scores.surprise_notes: Schema distance 6.5 — optimal zone. Violates golf's deepest belief ('it's the archer, not the arrow') with provable physics. Not so extreme that it confuses; surprising enough to create the 'wait, tell me more' response.
dimension_scores.narrative_reorganization_notes: Explains the golfer's ENTIRE putting history: every miss, every carousel purchase, every honeymoon that ended, every pressure putt that failed. 'The arrow has been bent this whole time' reorganizes past experience through a single lens. The past wasn't random bad luck or personal failure — it was a hidden physics flaw.
dimension_scores.mechanism_integration_notes: Forward Axis Weighting IS the straightened arrow. The mechanism is not bolted onto the Big Idea — it IS the resolution of the metaphor. FDB demo proves the arrow is now straight.
dimension_scores.promise_coherence_notes: When the arrow is straight, the archer's aim finally shows. 'Your putting matches the rest of your game' is the natural conclusion of the bent arrow being fixed.
dimension_scores.soul_md_compliance_notes: Quiet power. The reframe is delivered as a discovered truth. No hype, no 'game-changing' language. The metaphor does the work. Engineering authority validates the claim. Evolutionary narrative respects competitors.
dimension_scores.marketplace_reality_notes: No competitor can replicate this Big Idea because no competitor has Forward Axis Weighting with visual proof (FDB). The 'bent arrow' metaphor is ownable because only RS1 can demonstrate the 'straightened arrow.' LAB can claim zero torque but cannot claim the arrow was bent — their arrow hangs straight but still opens relative to the target line.

to_offer.big_idea_for_naming: The Bent Arrow / Let It Fall — offer naming should resonate with the gravity/straightening theme
to_offer.campaign_platform: Let it fall. / Do less. Make more. / Gravity-Driven. Face-Controlled.
to_offer.positioning_summary: RS1 is the straightened arrow — the first putter where gravity works with the face instead of against it

to_structure.argument_backbone: The arrow has been bent → here's the physics → here's the proof → here's what happens when it's straight → here's how to get one
to_structure.section_flow: Vindication hook → Root cause revelation → Mechanism explanation (Forward Axis Weighting) → Proof (FDB demo, McGinley, specs) → Promise (do less, make more) → Evolution story → Offer
to_structure.emotional_arc: Surprise/curiosity → Recognition/vindication → Understanding → Confidence → Resolution → Action
to_structure.three_audience_layers: Serious amateur (feel the vindication) → Low-handicap (verify the physics) → Gear enthusiast (analyze the engineering)

to_campaign_brief.big_idea_summary: The Bent Arrow: Golf's deepest belief ('it's the archer, not the arrow') is inverted by provable physics. Every putter has had the weight behind the shaft, creating face drift. RS1 moves it forward. The arrow is finally straight.
to_campaign_brief.creative_assets: 12 headlines, 3 full leads, campaign platform (tagline + product truth + supporting theme + big idea anchor)
to_campaign_brief.coherence_chain: Root cause (Rear Axis Weighting = face drift) → Mechanism (Forward Axis Weighting reverses physics) → Promise (putting matches ball-striking) → Big Idea (the arrow was bent this whole time) → Tagline (Let it fall) — all components thread through a single metaphor

primary_fssit.statement: Your putter has been fighting you your entire life...
primary_fssit.big_idea_delivery: The Bent Arrow directly crystallizes FSSIT-01 into a memorable metaphor. 'The arrow has been bent' = 'the putter has been fighting you.'

transformation_operators_applied: VILLAIN_REFRAME (Rear Axis Weighting as hidden antagonist)
schema_distance_analysis.resolution_accessibility: High — the FDB demo provides immediate physical proof that resolves the surprise. The golfer can see the straightened arrow in action.

narrative_reorganization_assessment.biographical_coherence: The bent arrow explains: (1) why every putter missed the same way, (2) why the carousel never ended, (3) why practice didn't transfer, (4) why pressure made it worse, (5) why the 'it's mental' explanation never quite fit. A single metaphor reorganizes the golfer's entire putting biography.
